title: Cat Vomiting Yellow Bile: Causes, When to Worry, and What to Do
meta_desc: Cat vomiting yellow bile? Learn common causes, if it's an emergency, and why a photo helps our vet team.
content_html:
TL;DR: Yellow vomit in cats usually indicates bile from an empty stomach and is typically a medium-urgency situation. If vomiting is frequent or accompanied by symptoms like lethargy and loss of appetite, you should contact a veterinarian promptly.

What does it mean if my cat is vomiting yellow liquid?

Vomiting yellow liquid in cats often indicates that their stomach is empty and they are expelling bile. Bile is a digestive fluid produced by the liver, and when it comes up, it usually means the cat has been feeling nauseous for some time, or has an empty stomach and is retching. While occasional vomiting of yellow liquid might not be a cause for alarm, persistent or frequent episodes warrant attention.

Is yellow vomit in cats considered a veterinary emergency?

  • Monitor your cat closely for any behavioral changes.
  • Observation is acceptable for a short period if the vomiting is infrequent and your cat is eating, drinking, and maintaining normal energy levels.
  • Seek veterinary advice promptly if vomiting is frequent, contains blood, or is accompanied by lethargy, loss of appetite, or diarrhea.
  • Be aware that dehydration can become a major concern with repeated vomiting episodes.

Why should I take a photo of my cat's vomit for the veterinary team?

  • It allows the veterinary team to assess the color and consistency of the vomit.
  • It helps identify the presence of any foreign material or blood.
  • Visual information combined with your description of symptoms helps provide an accurate initial assessment and determine the appropriate level of care.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

In cats, vomiting yellow bile can be associated with extrahepatic bile duct obstruction (EHBDO) or other hepatobiliary disorders. With EHBDO, vomiting may be episodic, and affected animals can exhibit inappetence or polyphagia, reflecting fat maldigestion due to a lack of enteric bile acids. Other signs of hepatobiliary disease include jaundice, hepatomegaly, and acholic feces. Concurrent pancreatitis is a poor prognostic indicator.

Chapter: Gastroenterology, Nutrition

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 439)

faqs.question: What does it mean when my cat vomits yellow liquid?
faqs.answer: Vomiting yellow liquid in cats typically means their stomach is empty and they are expelling bile. Bile is a digestive fluid that can irritate the stomach lining when present in excess or when the stomach is empty.

faqs.question: When should I be concerned about my cat vomiting yellow?
faqs.answer: You should be concerned if the vomiting is frequent, if your cat shows other signs of illness like lethargy, loss of appetite, diarrhea, or seems distressed. Vomiting that contains blood or looks like coffee grounds is also a sign to seek immediate veterinary attention.

faqs.question: Can an empty stomach cause a cat to vomit yellow?
faqs.answer: Yes, an empty stomach is a common reason for cats to vomit yellow bile. When there's no food to digest, the bile can irritate the stomach lining, triggering a vomiting reflex.

faqs.question: What are some potential causes of yellow vomit in cats?
faqs.answer: Potential causes include an empty stomach, dietary indiscretion (eating something unusual), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), pancreatitis, infections, foreign body ingestion, parasites, or even more serious conditions like kidney or liver disease. A vet can diagnose the specific cause.

faqs.question: What information should I provide the vet about my cat's vomit?
faqs.answer: It's helpful to provide the frequency of vomiting, what your cat has eaten recently, any changes in diet or environment, your cat's overall behavior (energy level, appetite), and the color/consistency of the vomit. A photo can also be very useful.
